rulings.Case Title: BRANDILYNN S. THOMAS VS. JACQUELYNN SMITH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, And Motion, To Set Aside Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 18, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT GLORIA THOMAS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT is continued to January 19, 2023. The Court is inclined to set this settlement aside. However, the Court will give Plaintiff an opportunity to file a motion to enforce the settlement and to enter a judgment per the settlement agreement as it appears from the opposition that Plaintiff believes that the settlement agreement is enforceable. Plaintiff shall file and properly notice Motion to Enforce to be heard on January 19, 2023. The Court notes that the moving party failed to cooperate with the probate proceedings and failed to address this failure in the reply. This persistent failure contradicts moving party's claim that she "remains interested in an informal resolution of this suit and will earnestly attempt one as soon as all proper persons are parties to the suit." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN SCHNEIDER VS. COCOA RESIDENTIAL OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 18, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and overruled in part. As to the second cause of action the demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer should be sustained with leave to amend to allege facts supporting the conclusory allegation regarding damages; demurrer should be otherwise overruled. As to the third cause of action demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions regarding damages. As to the fourth cause of action demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege an actionable cause of action under Subdivided Lands Act in good faith stating facts in support of each element of this cause of action including relief. The Court notes that Plaintiff cited no cases (other than discussing authorities cited by the Defendant) in its opposition pertaining to availably of a claim under the Subdivided Lands Act and expects the Plaintiff to only assert this cause of action in an amended complaint in good faith. It appears from the opposition that Plaintiff may want to assert a breach of CC&Rs claim arising out of the violation of the Subdivided Lands Act. Plaintiff may do so in the amended complaint if such allegations may be done in good faith. As to the fifth cause of action demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts showing that Plaintiff has already lost money or property. As to the sixth cause of action demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions to support each element of this cause of action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
